purpose: Lists past completed swarm runs sourced from {tracker_root}/history/*.json (via api.getHistory()) plus an optional analytics section computed by the !history --analytics command. Each entry shows status dot color (computed from overall_status / failed_tasks), task name, type badge, project badge, completion stats (e.g. '4/5 (1 failed)'), duration badge, and date prefix. AnalyticsSection renders aggregate metrics (total swarms, avg tasks, avg duration, failure rate, failure trend with color-coded label). Clicking an entry calls onItemClick(item) — typically restores the snapshot to a dashboard slot.
gotchas: Analytics is loaded via direct fetch('/api/history/analytics'), not the api.* IPC bridge — only works when the standalone HTTP server is reachable. In Electron-only mode this URL must be served by the embedded server or analytics will silently be null.

AnalyticsSection has THREE possible response shapes it tries to handle: bare object with total_swarms, wrapped { analytics: ... }, or null wrapper { analytics: null }. The branching logic is fragile if the API response shape changes.
When analytics is null, an instructional banner suggests running '!history --analytics' — but no UI button to trigger that command is provided.
Date string is item.cleared_at.slice(0, 10) — assumes ISO timestamp string format; non-ISO timestamps would render mojibake.
getDotColor: 'completed_with_errors' uses --color-blocked (yellow-ish) NOT --color-failed (red) — the warning vs error distinction is intentional but easy to miss.
patterns: Header comment 'Mirrors HistoryModal.js' — JSX-converted from older vanilla.
Three sub-components: AnalyticsSection (top), HistoryEntry (per row) — keeps render concerns separated.
Promise.all loads history + analytics in parallel.
conventions: Color tokens via CSS variables (--color-completed, --color-failed, --color-blocked, --color-accent, --color-type, --color-duration) rather than constants.js — chosen because all values flow through the theme system.
